name: Slow backend

injection.description: Degrade one backend so its TTFT and inter-token latency rise well above baseline (network latency injection or CPU/GPU throttling) while it keeps accepting requests.

expected_gateway_semantics: Pressure-aware routing shifts new requests away from the slow backend within a bounded interval (its in-flight/queue pressure is visible to routing).
Requests that exceed upstream deadlines fail typed (error.type=upstream_timeout) — never as untyped/generic internal errors.
The slow backend is not marked dead while it still completes requests; it receives proportionally less traffic.
expected_client_visible_behavior: Transient latency elevation for requests already routed to the slow backend, then recovery as routing shifts.
Deadline-exceeded requests surface a typed upstream_timeout error envelope with the request ID.

metrics_that_must_move.expectation: p99 rises for the injected backend label, then the injected backend's share of new requests falls
error_class=upstream_timeout increases for requests that hit the upstream deadline
rises on the slow backend (canonical key, resolved via the backend-capability name_mapping)

abort_condition: Abort the injection if aggregate goodput across the non-injected backends drops more than 20% below baseline for 120s (routing failed to shift), or if untyped 5xx errors appear.
